fields.label: Ledger Type
fields.description: Determines how this ledger captures changes. 'Track changes to a field' watches a single numeric field on the source DocType. ('Track balance from transactions' ships in a later release.)

fields.label: Is Active
fields.description: When unchecked, this configuration will not create ledger entries.

fields.label: Track Running Balance
fields.description: If unchecked, entries are created but no running balance is maintained.

fields.label: Source DocType
fields.description: The DocType whose field changes drive ledger entries.

fields.label: Track Value From
fields.options:
Field on document
Sum across child rows
fields.description: Most ledgers track a single field on the document. Pick 'Sum across child rows' only if the value is computed across a child table (e.g. total weight across line items).

fields.label: Child Table Field
fields.description: Pick the child table whose rows will be summed.

fields.label: Tracked Field
fields.description: The numeric field whose changes are logged. Must be Currency, Int, or Float.

fields.label: Posting Date & Time
fields.description: How should Ledgerly determine when each ledger entry was effective?

fields.label: Posting Date Source
fields.options:
Document modification time
Field on source DocType
fields.description: Pick where the posting datetime comes from. Use 'Document modification time' for masters that don't need retroactive dating. Use 'Field on source DocType' when the user-controlled measurement / transaction date matters (e.g. goat_weight_date, posting_date on a transaction).

fields.label: Posting Date Field
fields.description: A Date or Datetime field on the source DocType.

fields.label: Posting Time Field
fields.description: Optional: a Time field on the source DocType. Only useful when 'Posting Date Field' is a Date (not Datetime). If blank, time defaults to 00:00:00.

fields.label: Dimensions
fields.description: Link fields on the source DocType to use as reporting dimensions.
